stories.title: Applying Artificial Intelligence and machine learning in precision nutrition
stories.simple_summary: Scientists are studying how AI can help create personalized nutrition plans for people based on their individual health needs.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually help doctors give you better diet advice tailored to you, but it's still early research — nothing you need to do now.

stories.title: Revealed: landmark Scottish AI project has no prospect of meeting renewables promise
stories.simple_summary: A big AI project in Scotland promised to help with renewable energy but is falling behind and unlikely to deliver what was promised.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live in Scotland, this means a project you may have heard about won't deliver as hoped — mostly a concern for local government and energy planning.

stories.title: Microsoft Merges Enterprise and Consumer Copilot Apps
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft combined its ChatGPT-like tools (called Copilot) for businesses and regular people into one program.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Microsoft products, you might see fewer separate apps to choose from — a small change that makes things slightly simpler.

stories.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
stories.simple_summary: Some wealthy families are using AI instead of hiring traditional tutors or enrolling their children in regular schools.
stories.why_it_matters: For most families, traditional school is still the norm and what's available — this mostly affects very wealthy households experimenting with new options.

stories.title: The First Half of 2026 Is Over. These 2 Spectacular Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Can Soar in the Second Half.
stories.simple_summary: An investment website is recommending certain AI company stocks they think will perform well.
stories.why_it_matters: This is investment advice for stock traders — nothing you need to act on unless you're actively buying stocks.

stories.title: Global push for AI governance amid warnings of ‘catastrophic harm’
stories.simple_summary: World leaders are working together to create rules for how AI should be used safely, because people are worried AI could cause serious harm.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to everyone long-term — good safety rules now could prevent problems later, though changes may take years.

stories.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is closing its Mechanical Turk program, which was a website where people could do small tasks online for money.
stories.why_it_matters: If you used this as a side income, you'll need to find another option — otherwise, this doesn't affect most people.

stories.title: “Rubbish, it didn’t help at all”: Mick Jagger’s no fan of AI
stories.simple_summary: Mick Jagger, the famous rock singer, says AI-generated music isn't helpful and he doesn't like it.
stories.why_it_matters: This is one person's opinion about a celebrity topic — it doesn't change anything about how AI works or affect your life.

stories.title: China Insight: China’s Fashion Industry Enters the AI Era, From a Search Economy to Machine-led Discovery
stories.simple_summary: Chinese clothing companies are using AI to help customers discover products instead of relying on search tools.
stories.why_it_matters: If you shop from Chinese fashion companies, they might recommend items to you differently — a subtle change in how online shopping works.

stories.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made a commercial showing what it would look like if the founding fathers used Google's tools to write the Declaration of Independence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is marketing for Google's products — just an ad trying to be funny, nothing that affects how you work or live.

stories.title: Palo Alto Networks and Koi Security sued over alleged AI error in cyber threat report
stories.simple_summary: A company is being sued because AI gave a wrong answer in a cybersecurity report, potentially causing harm.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI can make mistakes that matter — mostly relevant to cybersecurity professionals and investors, though it highlights why you shouldn't trust AI completely.

stories.title: Philosophers Are the Latest Hiring Target for AI Companies
stories.simple_summary: AI companies are hiring philosophers (people who study thinking, logic, and ethics) to help them build better AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly interesting for job hunters in specialized fields — it shows companies care about ethics, but doesn't change AI tools you might use.
